authoritative_sources.title: FSMA Final Rule for Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's preventive-controls rule page is an authoritative public baseline for facilities that manufacture, process, pack, or hold food and it defines monitoring, corrective-action, records, and supply-chain expectations analytics must honor.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: source collection, metric definition, and quality or supplier boundary review
used_for: analytics escalation when food-safety posture changes

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 117 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 21 CFR Part 117 is the primary public regulatory baseline for cGMP, records, warehousing, distribution, supply-chain programs, and corrective-action posture in food CPG workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: source validation, release-boundary review, and handling of held or reworked product metrics

authoritative_sources.title: FSMA Final Rule on Requirements for Additional Traceability Records for Certain Foods
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's traceability-rule page is the authoritative public baseline for Critical Tracking Events and Key Data Elements that shape lot-aware analytics and recall exposure reporting for covered foods.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: traceability-aware analysis and recall exposure review

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Traceability Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized standards body for identifier and traceability exchange standards used across CPG supplier, plant, warehouse, distributor, and retailer workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 General Specifications Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 General Specifications are the core public standards reference for how identification keys and barcodes are applied across consumer, case, and higher packaging levels used in inventory and shipment analytics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: metric source mapping and packaging-hierarchy interpretation

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Logistic Label Guideline
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The GS1 Logistic Label Guideline is the recognized public operating reference for SSCC-based pallet and logistic-unit labeling, staging, and shipment matching in downstream CPG workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: Initiation of Voluntary Recalls Under 21 CFR Part 7, Subpart C
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA recall guidance is an authoritative operating reference for when analytical outputs must preserve hold, recall, and release-boundary uncertainty rather than implying safe movement or closure.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: quality hold, release-boundary, and recall escalation review
used_for: refusal to imply safe release during unresolved events

authoritative_sources.title: Guidance for Industry: Food Labeling Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's Food Labeling Guide is the public guidance baseline for food-label requirements and helps define where descriptive operational reporting ends and formal product-claim review begins.

authoritative_sources.title: Management's Discussion and Analysis, Selected Financial Data, and Supplementary Financial Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC MD&A guidance is the authoritative public baseline for how key performance indicators and operational metrics should be defined, contextualized, and caveated when used in public-company style disclosure.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: narrative synthesis, disclosure review, and caveat design
used_for: context and caveat requirements for disclosures

workflow_stages.name: source collection and validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm plant, line, product family, period, systems of record, refresh state, and authoritative source hierarchy before analysis starts.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: metric calculation and source definition
workflow_stages.description: Define the requested metric, denominator, exclusions, segmentation rules, and refresh assumptions while preserving held, reworked, released, and traceability-dependent states.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: traceability-aware reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Reconcile batch, lot, pallet, inventory, and destination records across ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, and supplier systems without collapsing conflicts.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: analysis or reporting package assembly
workflow_stages.description: Assemble the report, metric dictionary, narrative summary, confidence posture, and action thresholds with explicit evidence lineage and audience context.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: narrative synthesis and boundary escalation
workflow_stages.description: Review whether the output crosses into quality release, supplier approval, engineering judgment, or external disclosure authority and escalate rather than overstate.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

artifacts: production orders, batch or lot records, and line or plant segmentation records
inventory states, allocation or reservation records, pallet or shipment records, and logistics labels
quality holds, nonconformance reports, release records, deviation records, and rework status logs
supplier records, supplier incident logs, approved-supplier lists, substitution requests, and change-control documentation
traceability logs, lot genealogy maps, Critical Tracking Event records, and destination-node mappings
metric dictionaries, dashboard definitions, scorecards, threshold definitions, and reporting cadence schedules
customer, investor, or management reporting requests and publication approval notes

allowed: classify the request as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
request or validate the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source hierarchy required for the analysis
prepare evidence-backed metric definitions, analytical briefs, caveat notes, reconciliation notes, and threshold summaries without overstating authority
escalate: final quality release, hold disposition, quarantine release, or recall closure
supplier qualification, supplier substitution approval, or supplier nonconformance disposition
external disclosure signoff, legal interpretation, or policy-override decisions
refuse: publish or imply certainty when source quality, signoff, or metric definitions are unresolved
bypass traceability, hold, release, or quality controls to produce a cleaner metric
approve supplier, release, or engineering decisions because trends appear favorable
state unsupported causal, benchmark, or disclosure claims

domain_failure_modes: held or quarantined inventory is counted as released or available because release-boundary records were ignored
ERP, MES, WMS, and QMS conflicts are collapsed into a single metric without preserving source lineage
traceability analysis drops lot genealogy or pallet lineage and overstates containment or exposure confidence
supplier trend data is treated as approval authority for substitution or qualification decisions
unsupported causal or disclosure claims are presented without evidence or approval context
tenant-specific KPI overrides silently replace the public or historical baseline and create metric drift
the lane widens into quality, engineering, procurement, supplier-quality, or disclosure signoff authority

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority

unresolved_ambiguities.detail: The public baseline is anchored in FDA food-oriented CPG operations plus GS1 and SEC guidance. Tenants operating beauty, household, or other non-food lines must supply the applicable product-safety and claims overlays at runtime.

Public SEC guidance anchors KPI disclosure discipline, but private-company, customer-specific, or jurisdiction-specific reporting obligations still require tenant publication policy and approval-chain context.

notes: Authoritative batch, lot, inventory, supplier, and release records are the tenant-designated ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, supplier, planning, and logistics systems constrained by FDA, eCFR, and GS1 baselines where applicable.
The lane stops before formal quality release, supplier approval, engineering change approval, and final external disclosure signoff even when the analysis is operationally urgent.
